rows.notes: Clear, legible structural label at top of page. No monetary figures on the same line; treated as a header introducing the list of quitrents.
Description is legible; no amount clearly written on the same horizontal line (amount column appears blank/very faint).

Handwritten annotation includes numbers as part of the description; I cannot confidently separate these from columnar amounts. Left amounts blank due to ambiguity between descriptive numerals and the right-hand currency columns.
Short, clear entry name. No obvious amounts in the right-hand currency columns aligned to this line.

The description contains numeric figures (4.14.11) but the right-hand currency columns on this line are not unambiguously aligned to those figures; left blank to avoid misplacing descriptive numbers into currency columns.
Single-word place/name entry with no clear amounts in the currency columns on this line.
Legible name, no amount visible aligned to the row.
Clear name; currency columns on this line appear blank or faded.
The '10s' is written as part of the description rather than clearly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; column values are not unambiguous so amounts left blank.
Handwriting for the numeric part after Walker is smudged/faint; unsure whether those figures are descriptive or columnar. Chose to leave currency blank due to uncertainty.
Description legible; no clear amounts aligned with this row in the right-hand columns.
Short name, clear; right-hand currency columns blank on this line.
Legible place/name; no unambiguous currency figures on line.
Clear heading-like entry; currency columns empty for this line.
Numeric figures appear within the name/description; alignment to currency columns is ambiguous so amounts left blank.
Description contains numbers that could be internal notes rather than the right-hand £ s d columns; unclear alignment.
Legible place/name. On the page a brace groups Tintinhull with adjacent names; this line itself has no clear currency figures.
Part of a braced group (Tintinhull { Rush ... }); no distinct currency figures aligned to this specific line.
Second name in the brace group; no separate amount visible on the right-hand columns for this line.
Clear place name; no legible money figures in columns on same line.
Numeric cluster appears as part of description; I could not confidently map these digits into the standard £/s/d columns so left amount blank.
Single-word entry; currency columns for this row appear blank or extremely faint.
Place and tenure note is legible; no clear currency figures aligned horizontally.

rows.description: Cudlington Augm. Red. ad Fest. Purificacions
rows.notes: Ink is somewhat faded here and the long descriptive phrase contains abbreviations; the amount column for this line is unclear and possibly smudged.
There is a numeric amount nearby but the pence digit reads like '12' (invalid) so column alignment is ambiguous; left blank to avoid invalid currency extraction.
Following line to previous entry; any small numerals are faint and not unambiguously in the £/s/d columns.
Name legible; currency columns on that line are not clearly filled or are faded.

rows.description: Ladlane, mr Sparks and Meriton on Ann. 17-13-4
rows.notes: This line shows a clear monetary value in the right-hand columns (read as £17 13s 4d). Vertical alignment matches the three currency ruling lines; digits are distinct and valid.
Right-hand columns read clearly as 06 13 4 (interpreted as £6 13s 4d). Column alignment is consistent with prior line; shillings and pence fall in valid ranges.
The word 'Backgate' with an opening brace appears to introduce a grouped pair of lines below. No direct currency on this header line.
Text is legible but the currency columns for this line are faint/partly obscured; a small numeric cluster exists but is ambiguous whether it belongs to this line or the brace grouping.
Second braced item; corresponding amounts at the right are faint and partly obscured by smudge/shadow. I did not assign currency values for this specific row due to uncertainty.
A boxed subtotal/total at the right of the page reads as 125 10 10. Digits are clear though the last pence digit is slightly smudged; all currency parts are within valid ranges.

rows.description: Recd of mr verman for Batts due to ye Coll? and for so much pd to mr Berry ye Brewer
rows.notes: Receipt line in lower section; the right-hand columns read clearly as £13 17s 10d. Text is legible though a couple of abbreviations ('Batts', 'pd') are present.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins Bursar for Increments
rows.notes: Lower-section receipt; right-hand columns clearly read £12 12s 9d. Numerals are distinct and align with the column ruling lines.

rows.description: Recd. for Gent. Commoners of mr Vincent & mr Treby & mr Davie &c
rows.notes: Large receipt line; the right-hand columns read very clearly as £40 00s 00d. High contrast and alignment make this reading reliable.

rows.description: mr Vincent & mr Treby & mr Davie &c (continued grouping)
rows.notes: There is a continuation/annotation of the preceding line in the same horizontal area; no separate, distinct currency figure is unambiguously aligned to this continuation line.
Text reads 'mr Arscott & mr Baber 8t' and the nearby right-hand columns show '04 00 0' — interpreted as £4 0s 0d. The '8t' in the description is ambiguous but the currency cluster aligns clearly.

rows.description: mrs for Act supper; recd mr Lane 2t; mr Marker 2t
rows.notes: Several small notations for items and names; the adjacent currency cell (if any) is faint and possibly merged with nearby lines. Unable to confidently assign a currency value to this specific line.

rows.description: Ballts for Egges Saturday and Ap[ril] Wednesday
rows.notes: Below the 'Act supper' lines there is a grouped list (St Potbury, St Orchard, St Gee, St Pennington) with a clear right-hand figure '2-08-00' which I interpret as £2 8s 0d for these ballot/egg receipts. Alignment and digits are reasonably clear.

rows.description: St Potbury 2t St Orchard 2t St Gee 2t St Pennington 2t (grouped items)
rows.notes: These four parish/items are listed together as the source for the previously read £2 8s 0d; their individual contributions are not separated on the page, so per-line currency is not present.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for ye Commons Library
rows.notes: This line has been crossed out on the original page; a faint numeric '0.8.0' (8 shillings) appears but crossing-out and smearing reduce confidence. Pounds digit is not readable (assumed 0).

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for increments for ye year July 16th 1701
rows.notes: The line references the year and shows a numeric cluster to the right reading '27 2 ?' — the pence digit is faint or partially obscured. I read it as £27 2s 0d but pence is uncertain.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for ye Comons Library (another receipt)
rows.notes: Near the bottom is another receipt line with a numeric cluster that appears to read '29 00 0' (interpreted as £29 0s 0d). The line is slightly faded and crosses/overwritings nearby reduce certainty.
Final bottom-right numeric total reads as '115 00' or '115 00 0'. The digits are visible but slightly cut off by the page edge and shadow; interpreted as £115 0s 0d.
